prose:
Method provenance (frontier-orbital gap, water)

• Property: frontier orbital energies and HOMO–LUMO gap (Koopmans-style orbital energy difference), eV
• Level of theory: DFT, B3LYP/def2-TZVP (exact two-electron integrals; density fitting not enabled)
• Geometry: user-supplied Cartesian coordinates (/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/water/water.xyz); single-point calculation ‑ geometry not re-optimized
• Environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent)
• Charge 0, multiplicity 1 (closed-shell singlet)
• Software: PySCF (via chemkit frontier-orbitals skill)
• Convergence: SCF converged in 7 cycles; integrity.status = "ok", trustworthy = true; no warnings reported.
• Live log: streamed while running to a frontier_<timestamp>.out file in the working directory; full JSON result at /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmptrhlirqp/live.json

Key numerical results (eV):
  HOMO  = ‑8.6037183
  LUMO  =  +0.33695037
  HOMO–LUMO gap = 8.9406687   ← headline value

Command used (reproducibility):
/ home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py frontier --method dft --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/water/water.xyz --out /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmptrhlirqp/live.json

This is a computed single-point DFT result; orbital gaps are method-dependent and differ from true excitation energies.
